pir: Will the tracked competitor make a material pricing change within 30 days, and if so in which direction and on which product line?

ground_truth.state: Cirrus has, in fact, decided to cut its Starter plan price within the window. The available signal stream is evenly split: three signals are accurate, concrete, and directly observed, and genuinely support the cut, while three other signals are inaccurate, hedged, secondhand, and unconfirmed, and point toward a false Enterprise price increase that is not actually happening. Both clusters carry broadly comparable declared source reliability, so a naive vote count would treat them as a tie. The correct read requires weighing the content and credibility of each cluster rather than its raw count: the cut cluster rests on direct observation and a first-hand briefing, while the increase cluster rests on secondhand rumor, an unconfirmed and unverifiable document, and an inferred rather than confirmed connection. The material action in the window is a price cut on the Starter line.

signal_stream.content: A reliable contact in Cirrus sales reports the team was briefed this week on a new, lower Starter-tier price set to take effect at the start of next month.
A cached version of the Cirrus pricing page, surfaced through their CDN, shows the Starter plan at a lower price than currently listed; the page is staged and not yet linked from the live site.
On a podcast, the Cirrus CEO said the company wants to lower the barrier to entry and make it easier for smaller teams to get started.
A contact who sometimes hears finance-related rumors mentioned secondhand that an Enterprise price increase might be under discussion, though they had no direct visibility into any decision.
A document circulating among partners, described as an updated enterprise order form, shows higher list rates than current public pricing; its origin and currency could not be confirmed.

An analyst report cites unnamed sources suggesting Cirrus may be adjusting sales compensation in a way that could imply higher Enterprise pricing, though the report acknowledges the connection is inferred rather than confirmed.
